segments.text: Sing aloud to God our strength;
Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob.
Raise a song and strike the timbrel, The pleasant harp with the lute.
Blow the trumpet at the time of the New Moon, At the full moon, on our solemn feast day.
For this is a statute for Israel, A law of the God of Jacob.
This He established in Joseph as a testimony, When He went throughout the land of Egypt, Where I heard a language I did not understand.
I removed his shoulder from the burden; His hands were freed from the baskets.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You called in trouble, and I delivered you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hear, O My people, and I will admonish you! O Israel, if you will listen to Me!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There shall be no foreign god among you; Nor shall you worship any foreign god.
I am the Lord your God, Who brought you out of the land of Egypt; Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But My people would not heed My voice, And Israel would have none of Me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I gave them over to their own stubborn heart, To walk in their own counsels.
Oh, that My people would listen to Me, That Israel would walk in My ways!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I would soon subdue their enemies, And turn My hand against their adversaries.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The haters of the Lord would pretend submission to Him, But their fate would endure forever.
He would have fed them also with the finest of wheat; And with honey from the rock I would have satisfied you.
segments.speaker: God
